lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਰਿਜੁ ਕਰਿ ਦਾਹਿਣੈ ਸਿਧਿ ਸਨਮੁਖ ਮੁਖੁ ਜੋਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: baarij kar daahinai sidh sanamukh mukh jovai |
lines.english: On His right hand is the sign of the lotus; the Siddhis, the supernatural spiritual powers, await His Command.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਕਯਥੁ ਜਨਮੁ ਕਲੵੁਚਰੈ ਗੁਰੁ ਪਰਸੵਿਉ ਅਮਰ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸੁ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: sakayath janam kalayucharai gur parasayiau amar pragaas |8|
lines.english: So speaks KALL: fruitful is the life of one who meets with Guru Amar Daas, radiant with the Light of God. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਿਦੈ ਬਸੈ ਅਕਹੀਉ ਸੋਇ ਰਸੁ ਤਿਨ ਹੀ ਜਾਤਉ ॥
lines.translit: ridai basai akaheeo soe ras tin hee jaatau |
lines.english: The Inexpressible Lord abides in His Heart; He alone knows this joy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਿਧਿ ਬਸੈ ਬਾਂਵਾਂਗਿ ਜੁ ਤੀਨਿ ਲੋਕਾਂਤਰ ਮੋਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: ridh basai baanvaang ju teen lokaantar mohai |
lines.english: On His left are worldly powers, which fascinate the three worlds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਖਹੁ ਭਗਤਿ ਉਚਰੈ ਅਮਰੁ ਗੁਰੁ ਇਤੁ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਾਤਉ ॥
lines.translit: mukhahu bhagat ucharai amar gur it rang raatau |
lines.english: Guru Amar Daas utters the words of devotion, imbued with the Love of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਸਤਕਿ ਨੀਸਾਣੁ ਸਚਉ ਕਰਮੁ ਕਲੵ ਜੋੜਿ ਕਰ ਧੵਾਇਅਉ ॥
lines.translit: masatak neesaan schau karam kalay jorr kar dhayaaeaau |
lines.english: On His forehead is the true insignia of the Lord's Mercy; with his palms pressed together, KALL meditates on Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਸਿਅਉ ਗੁਰੂ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਤਿਲਕੁ ਸਰਬ ਇਛ ਤਿਨਿ ਪਾਇਅਉ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: parasiaau guroo satigur tilak sarab ichh tin paaeaau |9|
lines.english: Whoever meets with the Guru, the certified True Guru, has all his desires fulfilled. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਣ ਤ ਪਰ ਸਕਯਥ ਚਰਣ ਗੁਰ ਅਮਰ ਪਵਲਿ ਰਯ ॥
lines.translit: charan ta par sakayath charan gur amar paval ray |
lines.english: Supremely fruitful are the feet which walk upon the path of Guru Amar Daas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਹ ਤ ਪਰ ਸਕਯਥ ਜੀਹ ਗੁਰ ਅਮਰੁ ਭਣਿਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: jeeh ta par sakayath jeeh gur amar bhanijai |
lines.english: Supremely fruitful is the tongue which utters the praises of Guru Amar Daas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਥ ਤ ਪਰ ਸਕਯਥ ਹਥ ਲਗਹਿ ਗੁਰ ਅਮਰ ਪਯ ॥
lines.translit: hath ta par sakayath hath lageh gur amar pay |
lines.english: Supremely fruitful are the hands which touch the feet of Guru Amar Daas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸ੍ਰਵਣ ਤ ਪਰ ਸਕਯਥ ਸ੍ਰਵਣਿ ਗੁਰੁ ਅਮਰੁ ਸੁਣਿਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: sravan ta par sakayath sravan gur amar sunijai |
lines.english: Supremely fruitful are the ears which hear the Praises of Guru Amar Daas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨੈਣ ਤ ਪਰ ਸਕਯਥ ਨਯਣਿ ਗੁਰੁ ਅਮਰੁ ਪਿਖਿਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: nain ta par sakayath nayan gur amar pikhijai |
lines.english: Supremely fruitful are the eyes which behold Guru Amar Daas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਕਯਥੁ ਸੁ ਸਿਰੁ ਜਾਲਪੁ ਭਣੈ ਜੁ ਸਿਰੁ ਨਿਵੈ ਗੁਰ ਅਮਰ ਨਿਤ ॥੧॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: sakayath su sir jaalap bhanai ju sir nivai gur amar nit |1|10|
lines.english: Fruitful is the head, says Jaalap, which bows forever before Guru Amar Daas. ||1||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਕਯਥੁ ਸੁ ਹੀਉ ਜਿਤੁ ਹੀਅ ਬਸੈ ਗੁਰ ਅਮਰਦਾਸੁ ਨਿਜ ਜਗਤ ਪਿਤ ॥
lines.translit: sakayath su heeo jit heea basai gur amaradaas nij jagat pit |
lines.english: Fruitful is the heart in which Guru Amar Daas, the Father of the world, Himself abides.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨਰ ਦੁਖ ਨਹ ਭੁਖ ਤਿਨਰ ਨਿਧਨ ਨਹੁ ਕਹੀਅਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tinar dukh neh bhukh tinar nidhan nahu kaheeeh |
lines.english: They do not suffer pain or hunger, and they cannot be called poor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨਰ ਸੇਵ ਨਹੁ ਕਰਹਿ ਤਿਨਰ ਸਯ ਸਹਸ ਸਮਪਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tinar sev nahu kareh tinar say sehas samapeh |
lines.english: They do not serve anyone else, but they give gifts to hundreds and thousands.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨਰ ਸੋਕੁ ਨਹੁ ਹੂਐ ਤਿਨਰ ਸੇ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਲਹੀਅਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tinar sok nahu hooai tinar se ant na laheeeh |
lines.english: They do not grieve, and their limits cannot be found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਖ ਲਹਹਿ ਤਿਨਰ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਮਹਿ ਅਭੈ ਪਟੁ ਰਿਪ ਮਧਿ ਤਿਹ ॥
lines.translit: sukh laheh tinar sansaar meh abhai patt rip madh tih |
lines.english: They find peace in this world, and live fearlessly amidst their enemies.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨਰ ਦੁਲੀਚੈ ਬਹਹਿ ਤਿ ਨਰ ਉਥਪਿ ਬਿਥਪਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tinar duleechai baheh ti nar uthap bithapeh |
lines.english: They sit on beautiful carpets; they establish and disestablish at will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਕਯਥ ਤਿਨਰ ਜਾਲਪੁ ਭਣੈ ਗੁਰ ਅਮਰਦਾਸੁ ਸੁਪ੍ਰਸੰਨੁ ਜਿਹ ॥੨॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: sakayath tinar jaalap bhanai gur amaradaas suprasan jih |2|11|
lines.english: They are fruitful and prosperous, says Jaalap. Guru Amar Daas is pleased with them. ||2||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਪਢਿਅਉ ਇਕੁ ਮਨਿ ਧਰਿਅਉ ਇਕੁ ਕਰਿ ਇਕੁ ਪਛਾਣਿਓ ॥
lines.translit: tai padtiaau ik man dhariaau ik kar ik pachhaanio |
lines.english: You read about the One Lord, and enshrine Him in Your mind; You realize the One and Only Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਯਣਿ ਬਯਣਿ ਮੁਹਿ ਇਕੁ ਇਕੁ ਦੁਹੁ ਠਾਂਇ ਨ ਜਾਣਿਓ ॥
lines.translit: nayan bayan muhi ik ik duhu tthaane na jaanio |
lines.english: With Your eyes and the words You speak, You dwell upon the One Lord; You do not know any other place of rest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਪਨਿ ਇਕੁ ਪਰਤਖਿ ਇਕੁ ਇਕਸ ਮਹਿ ਲੀਣਉ ॥
lines.translit: supan ik paratakh ik ikas meh leenau |
lines.english: You know the One Lord while dreaming, and the One Lord while awake. You are absorbed in the One.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਸ ਇਕੁ ਅਰੁ ਪੰਜਿ ਸਿਧੁ ਪੈਤੀਸ ਨ ਖੀਣਉ ॥
lines.translit: tees ik ar panj sidh paitees na kheenau |
lines.english: At the age of seventy-one, You began to march towards the Indestructible Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਹੁ ਜਿ ਲਾਖੁ ਲਖਹੁ ਅਲਖੁ ਹੈ ਇਕੁ ਇਕੁ ਕਰਿ ਵਰਨਿਅਉ ॥
lines.translit: eikahu ji laakh lakhahu alakh hai ik ik kar varaniaau |
lines.english: The One Lord, who takes hundreds of thousands of forms, cannot be seen. He can only be described as One.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਅਮਰਦਾਸ ਜਾਲਪੁ ਭਣੈ ਤੂ ਇਕੁ ਲੋੜਹਿ ਇਕੁ ਮੰਨਿਅਉ ॥੩॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: gur amaradaas jaalap bhanai too ik lorreh ik maniaau |3|12|
lines.english: So speaks Jaalap: O Guru Amar Daas, You long for the One Lord, and believe in the One Lord alone. ||3||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿ ਮਤਿ ਗਹੀ ਜੈਦੇਵਿ ਜਿ ਮਤਿ ਨਾਮੈ ਸੰਮਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: ji mat gahee jaidev ji mat naamai samaanee |
lines.english: The understanding which Jai Dayv grasped, the understanding which permeated Naam Dayv,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿ ਮਤਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਲੋਚਨ ਚਿਤਿ ਭਗਤ ਕੰਬੀਰਹਿ ਜਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: ji mat trilochan chit bhagat kanbeereh jaanee |
lines.english: the understanding which was in the consciousness of Trilochan and known by the devotee Kabeer,

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੁਕਮਾਂਗਦ ਕਰਤੂਤਿ ਰਾਮੁ ਜੰਪਹੁ ਨਿਤ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: rukamaangad karatoot raam janpahu nit bhaaee |
lines.english: by which Rukmaangad constantly meditated on the Lord, O Siblings of Destiny,

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮਰੀਕਿ ਪ੍ਰਹਲਾਦਿ ਸਰਣਿ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਗਤਿ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: amareek prahalaad saran gobind gat paaee |
lines.english: which brought Ambreek and Prahlaad to seek the Sanctuary of the Lord of the Universe, and which brought them to salvation

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਲੋਭੁ ਕ੍ਰੋਧੁ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਤਜੀ ਸੁ ਮਤਿ ਜਲੵ ਜਾਣੀ ਜੁਗਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: tai lobh krodh trisanaa tajee su mat jalay jaanee jugat |
lines.english: says JALL that sublime understanding has brought You to renounce greed, anger and desire, and to know the way.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਅਮਰਦਾਸੁ ਨਿਜ ਭਗਤੁ ਹੈ ਦੇਖਿ ਦਰਸੁ ਪਾਵਉ ਮੁਕਤਿ ॥੪॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: gur amaradaas nij bhagat hai dekh daras paavau mukat |4|13|
lines.english: Guru Amar Daas is the Lord's own devotee; gazing upon the Blessed Vision of His Darshan, one is liberated. ||4||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਅਮਰਦਾਸੁ ਪਰਸੀਐ ਪੁਹਮਿ ਪਾਤਿਕ ਬਿਨਾਸਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur amaradaas paraseeai puham paatik binaaseh |
lines.english: Meeting with Guru Amar Daas, the earth is purged of its sin.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਅਮਰਦਾਸੁ ਪਰਸੀਐ ਸਿਧ ਸਾਧਿਕ ਆਸਾਸਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur amaradaas paraseeai sidh saadhik aasaaseh |
lines.english: The Siddhas and seekers long to meet with Guru Amar Daas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਅਮਰਦਾਸੁ ਪਰਸੀਐ ਅਭਉ ਲਭੈ ਗਉ ਚੁਕਿਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur amaradaas paraseeai abhau labhai gau chukihi |
lines.english: Meeting with Guru Amar Daas, the Fearless Lord is obtained, and the cycle of reincarnation is brought to an end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਅਮਰਦਾਸੁ ਪਰਸੀਐ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਲਹੀਐ ਪਉ ਮੁਕਿਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur amaradaas paraseeai dhiaan laheeai pau mukihi |
lines.english: Meeting with Guru Amar Daas, the mortal meditates on the Lord, and his journey comes to its end.
